commerce.tagline: 250-BBL stainless mash tun with steam jacket + bottom-mounted agitator
commerce.description: The Cascade 250-BBL Industrial Mash Tun is a stainless-steel infusion mash tun with a steam-jacketed bottom, false-bottom screen, and a variable-speed agitator. Sized for the canonical 250-BBL industrial brewhouse cycle (4-hour mash + lauter). Targets regional craft + production tenants brewing 5-10 daily turns.

common_failure_modes.name: Agitator gearbox seal leak
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Drain mash, replace mechanical seal, refill grease, run dry-cycle test.

common_failure_modes.name: False-bottom blockage (stuck mash)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Underback rake-and-rinse; if persistent, pull and acid-wash the screen.

common_failure_modes.name: Steam jacket low-pressure
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Inspect trap + bypass valve; pressure-test jacket once isolated.

pm_checklist: Check steam jacket pressure relief valve
Replace mash paddle wear bushings if >50% worn
CIP cycle the vessel + verify clean discharge

commerce.tagline: 250-BBL lauter tun with motorized rakes + grant collection
commerce.description: Stainless lauter tun with a motorized rake assembly, false-bottom screen, and a grant for runoff collection. Sized for the 250-BBL brewhouse — supports 90-minute lauter cycles with vorlauf recirculation.

common_failure_modes.name: Rake motor amperage spike
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Inspect for stuck rakes; reduce rake depth; if persistent, swap motor brushes.

common_failure_modes.name: Grant pump cavitation
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Adjust runoff rate; verify suction line not air-locked.

physical.power_requirements: Natural gas burner, 6.5M BTU/hr — separate gas service

commerce.tagline: 250-BBL direct-fire kettle with internal whirlpool + hop addition port
commerce.description: Stainless brew kettle with direct-fired natural-gas burner, internal whirlpool function, and a top-mounted hop addition port for late + whirlpool hopping. 60-90 minute boil cycle at 250 BBL working volume.

common_failure_modes.name: Burner pilot dropout
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Clean pilot orifice; verify gas pressure; replace thermocouple if fault recurs.

common_failure_modes.name: Whirlpool inlet clog
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Pull inlet plate, clear hop debris, re-seal.

common_failure_modes.name: Sight-glass fouling
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Caustic + acid CIP; replace gasket if leaking after re-tighten.

pm_checklist: Run combustion analyzer + tune air/fuel ratio
Inspect whirlpool inlet for hop debris

physical.power_requirements: No electrical — gravity-fed wort + pumped glycol

commerce.tagline: Two-stage wort heat exchanger (city water + glycol) for 60-bbl/hr knockout
commerce.description: Plate-and-frame heat exchanger sized for 60 BBL/hr knockout. Two stages: city water pre-cools wort from boil temp to ~80F, glycol second stage drops wort to pitch temp (60-65F). Drop-out plate count tunable for seasonal water-temperature swings.

common_failure_modes.name: Gasket leak between plates
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Disassemble plate stack; replace EPDM gaskets in affected channels.

common_failure_modes.name: Plate fouling (reduced ΔT)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Caustic CIP at higher concentration + extended dwell; if plates pitted, replace stack.

pm_checklist: Verify ΔT vs design at standard flow rate

physical.power_requirements: Glycol-jacketed (no direct power); 24V solenoid for valve actuation

commerce.tagline: 30-BBL conical fermenter with dual glycol zones + dump port
commerce.description: Stainless cylindro-conical fermentation vessel, 30-BBL working volume (35-BBL gross). Two independently-controlled glycol zones (cone + body), bottom dump for yeast harvest, racking arm for clear-beer transfer. Standard pressure rating 15 PSI.

common_failure_modes.name: Glycol jacket leak
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Pressure-test jacket; if leak isolated to one zone, isolate that zone + run on backup.

common_failure_modes.name: Dump valve seal weep
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace tri-clamp gasket; if valve seat damaged, swap valve assembly.

common_failure_modes.name: Pressure relief valve sticking
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Pull PRV, clean spring + seat, re-test crack pressure on bench.

commerce.tagline: 30-BBL horizontal brite tank for carbonation + cold-conditioning
commerce.description: Stainless horizontal brite/serving tank, 30-BBL working volume. Used for natural carbonation, cold-conditioning, and the bridging hold between fermentation and packaging. Lower height than FVs to fit cellar racks; gas-injection carbonation stone optional.

common_failure_modes.name: Carb stone clogging
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Remove stone, sonic-clean in citric acid bath; replace if dwell time hasn't recovered.

commerce.tagline: 30-ton dual-compressor glycol chiller — fermentation + brite cooling
commerce.description: 30-ton (105 kW) industrial glycol chiller with dual 15HP scroll compressors, 200-gal insulated reservoir, and twin pump trains. Provides chilled glycol to all fermentation + brite tanks plus the wort heat exchanger second stage. Lead-lag compressor staging.

common_failure_modes.name: Compressor short-cycling
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Check refrigerant charge; verify low-pressure cutout setpoint; inspect contactor for chatter.

common_failure_modes.name: Glycol pump seal failure
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Isolate pump, replace mechanical seal, refill loop, re-bleed.

common_failure_modes.name: Reservoir glycol concentration drift
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Test concentration with refractometer; top up with concentrate to maintain 35% propylene glycol.

physical.power_requirements: Natural gas, 1.7M BTU/hr; 480V 3-phase, 15A for blower + controls

commerce.tagline: 50-HP gas-fired packaged steam boiler — mash tun + kettle service
commerce.description: Hurst 50-HP packaged steam boiler with 150-PSI design rating (operates ~30 PSI). Provides steam to the mash tun jacket, kettle (when retrofit with steam coil), and ancillary CIP heating. Annual ASME inspection required.

common_failure_modes.name: Low-water cutoff stuck
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Drain LWCO chamber, manually verify float travel; if seized, swap LWCO assembly.

common_failure_modes.name: Burner soot buildup
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Vacuum tubes + firebox; verify air/fuel ratio with combustion analyzer; tune burner head.

physical.power_requirements: 208V 1-phase, 30A; compressed air 100 PSI; CIP chemical feeds

commerce.tagline: Single-head keg wash + fill — 25 kegs/hr at 1/2-BBL or 1/6-BBL
commerce.description: Combination keg washer + filler. 5-stage wash cycle (water purge, caustic, intermediate rinse, acid sanitizer, sterile water + steam) followed by counter-pressure fill from a brite tank. Throughput ~25 kegs/hr at 1/2-BBL; ~40/hr at 1/6-BBL.

common_failure_modes.name: Spear seal leak (keg coupler)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace coupler O-ring stack; verify keg spear isn't out-of-spec.

common_failure_modes.name: Caustic dosing pump drift
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Calibrate dosing pump against measured-volume test; replace diaphragm if worn.

pm_checklist: Verify steam sterilization temp at filler head

physical.power_requirements: 208V 3-phase, 50A; CO2 + N2 service; compressed air 90 PSI

commerce.tagline: 4-head mobile canning line, 30 cans/min for 12oz aluminum
commerce.description: 4-head purge-fill-seam canning line on a mobile chassis. Pre-purges cans with CO2 to reduce dissolved-O2 pickup, fills under counter-pressure, seams the lid in line. Produces 30 cans/min sustained at 12oz format; 22 cans/min at 16oz.

common_failure_modes.name: Seamer roll wear (low-double-seam)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Tear-down seam check; replace 1st-op or 2nd-op seamer roll; recalibrate.

common_failure_modes.name: Fill-head O-ring failure
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Swap O-rings on affected head; verify no fill-tube damage.

common_failure_modes.name: DO pickup spike (>50 ppb)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Re-tune CO2 purge pressure + dwell; check fill-tube seal.

commerce.tagline: 53-gal new American oak, char level 3, for primary oak-aging
commerce.description: Standard 53-gallon American oak barrel, char level 3 (medium-plus toast). Imparts vanilla + coconut + caramel notes; suitable for first-fill stout, barleywine, or sour-aging programs that want pronounced new-oak character. Single-use is the canonical lifecycle for new char-3; subsequent fills become 'refill oak' with diminished extraction.

common_failure_modes.name: Stave seepage (slow leak)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Re-soak barrel, drive bung tighter; if seepage persists at hoop line, retire to second-fill or compost.

common_failure_modes.name: Brett contamination (unintended)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Quarantine barrel for sour program or destroy; cannot be reliably sanitized for clean fermentation.

common_failure_modes.name: Excessive oxidation during aging
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Top off with same-batch beer monthly; verify bung seal; reduce ullage.

pm_checklist: Visual inspection for stave seepage at heads + bilge
Top-off check (replace headspace loss with same-batch beer)
Sample for taste + DO every 90 days

commerce.tagline: Used 53-gal ex-bourbon barrel, ~3yr fill, residual spirit ~2L
commerce.description: Standard 53-gallon American oak barrel previously used for ~3 years of bourbon aging per federal regulation. Arrives wet with residual bourbon (~2L), which influences the first beer fill (typical pickup: 0.4-0.7% ABV bump + bourbon character). Canonical vessel for imperial stout barrel-aging programs; expected to deliver 2-3 useful beer fills before character drops below threshold.

common_failure_modes.name: Stave seepage at bilge
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Re-soak with hot water; drive bung deeper; if persistent, retire after current fill.

common_failure_modes.name: Bourbon character exhausted (3rd+ fill)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Reclassify barrel for refill-oak program or sell to sour-aging tenant; no longer suitable for marketed bourbon-barrel-aged claims.

common_failure_modes.name: Brett or wild-yeast contamination
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Quarantine for sour program; do not return to clean-fermentation pool.

pm_checklist: Sample for taste + DO every 90 days
Track fill count (1/2/3) for character-budget reporting

commerce.tagline: Used 60-gal French oak ex-wine barrel, sour-program vessel
commerce.description: 60-gallon French oak barrel previously used for red-wine aging (typical: 2-3 vintages). Arrives with residual wine character + the indigenous microflora (Brettanomyces, Pediococcus, Lactobacillus) that resident wine programs cultivate. The canonical vessel for wild + sour aging in the brewery: tannins from French oak, fruit notes from wine, microbiome for slow secondary fermentation. Lifecycle is years, not months — expect 4-7 fills over 3-6 years before character flattens.

common_failure_modes.name: Hoop loosening (over years)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Drive hoop tighter with cooper's hammer; if iron is rusted-through, retire.

common_failure_modes.name: Acetic spike (excess oxygen)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Top off aggressively, verify bung seal; if VA exceeds threshold, blend off or destroy.

common_failure_modes.name: Microbial drift (off-character)
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Sample + sensory panel; if irrecoverable, retire from sour program.

pm_checklist: Visual inspection for stave + hoop integrity
Top-off check (more frequent for sours — ullage drives VA)
Sample for sensory + VA every 180 days
Track fill count + age for terroir-reporting
